item.title: Turkish national among 10 killed in Pak gun attack
item.description: A group of militants killed at least 10 people, including a Turkish national, at a copper and gold mining project in Pakistan’s southwestern Balochistan province, officials said on Thursday, with another Turk taken hostage....Sports news updates

item.title: Cabinet scraps duty-free car privilege for MPs
item.description: The cabinet has given final approval to a draft law abolishing the long-standing duty-free car import facility for the members of the Jatiya Sangssad in a move reflecting the country’s current economic realities....

item.title: TONU MURDER: PBI collects DNA sample of ex-army warrant officer Hafizur
item.description: The Police Bureau of Investigation has collected a DNA sample of former Bangladesh Army Senior Warrant Officer Hafizur Rahman in connection with the murder of Cumilla Victoria College student and cultural activist Sohagi Jahan Tonu....

item.title: Consumers still suffer despite increased fuel oil supply
item.description: Consumers in the capital Dhaka continued to struggle to collect fuel oil from crowded filling stations for the 48th consecutive day on Thursday, despite a slight improvement in supplies from government depots....

item.title: Progress made, challenges remain in RMG sector
item.description: The multi-billion-dollar readymade garment industry of Bangladesh has undergone sweeping reforms to improve workplace safety and restore global confidence in the industry since the Rana Plaza collapse, one of the deadliest man-made industrial accidents in history. ...Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: Load-shedding reaches 3,000MW
item.description: The country on Thursday faced a power generation shortfall of around 3,000 megawatts due to a shortage of primary energy, exacerbated by the prolonged conflict in the Persian Gulf....

item.title: Justice still elusive 13 years after Rana Plaza collapse
item.description: Thirteen years after the catastrophic collapse of Rana Plaza, one of Bangladesh’s deadliest man-made industrial disasters, justice and compensation for victims remain largely unresolved....

item.title: 13 YEARS OF RANA PLAZA COLLAPSE: Justice still elusive
item.description: Thirteen years after the catastrophic collapse of Rana Plaza, one of Bangladesh’s deadliest man-made industrial disasters, justice and compensation for victims remain largely unresolved....

item.title: Israel’s war obsession
item.description: IT IS tempting to argue that Israel’s new military doctrine is predicated on perpetual war — but the reality is more complex......

item.title: Asymmetric deterrence
item.description: GEOPOLITICONOMY is not a theory waiting to be tested. It is a reality already unfolding. Recent conflicts have revealed a profound shift in how power is exercised and, more importantly, how it is deterred. The classical architecture of deterrence, built on nuclear parity and mutually assured destruction, is no longer the sole pillar sustaining global stability. What has......

item.title: Myth of just transition
item.description: THE climate crisis is accelerating, yet public support for climate action is fraying under the weight of rising living costs and deepening economic insecurity. Around the world, people are being asked to shoulder transitions they neither designed nor meaningfully benefit from. If climate policies continue to ignore everyday realities, especially those of women, workers and......

item.title: Reward or responsibility?
item.description: ON APRIL 20, BNP named 36 women for parliament’s reserved seats. The Jamaat-led alliance named 13. With that, one of the most momentous decisions about women’s representation in Bangladesh’s new parliament was made — quietly, quickly and with far less public scrutiny than it deserves......Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: Eviction drives alone can’t resolve terminal disorder
item.description: THE photograph that New Age published on April 23 shows long-route buses parked in multiple rows along the busy road passing by the Mahakhali inter-district bus terminal, reducing the effective carriageway to a narrow passage for moving vehicles. The photograph depicts what has effectively become a routine arrangement rather than an exceptional occurrence, where......

item.title: No white elephant out of Maheskhali mooring
item.description: THE Maheskhali Single Point Mooring, constructed to the west of the island in Cox’s Bazar under a project initially involving a cost of Tk 49.36 billion that finally reached Tk 83.41 billion after a series of revisions, has been lying idle for about two years as the government has failed to find an operator. The mooring, which China Petroleum Engineering Pipeline Co began......

item.title: EU signs off on Ukraine loan, new Russia sanctions
item.description: The EU on Thursday gave final approval to a 90-billion-euro ($105 billion) loan for Ukraine and a new round of sanctions on Russia, in a boost for Kyiv after a prolonged row....Sports news updates

item.title: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu cast votes
item.description: Voting began Thursday in two of India’s politically key opposition-held states, with tens of millions casting ballots in West Bengal and the southern Tamil Nadu, reports Agence France-Presse ....

item.title: Rohingya boat exodus surges by 90pc: Save the Children
item.description: The number of Rohingyas fleeing Bangladesh and Myanmar by boat has increased by 90 per cent compared with last year, Save the Children said on Thursday, based on United Nations figures....

item.title: Speaker urges MPs to follow etiquette, traditions
item.description: Speaker Hafiz Uddin Ahmed on Thursday issued a ruling in the Jatiya Sangsad, expressing concern over repeated breaches of decorum during sessions and urging lawmakers to strictly observe parliamentary etiquette and procedures....

item.title: Jamaat MP pays tribute to Sheikh Mujib, Ghulam Azam in JS
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami lawmaker Md Abdul Wares paid tribute to the country’s founding president Sheikh Mujibur Rahman in the Jatiya Sangsad and also remembered several late leaders of his party, including Ghulam Azam....Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: Legal notice served on Jamaat MP Amir Hamza
item.description: A legal notice has been served on Kushtia-3 lawmaker and Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami leader Amir Hamza for allegedly making defamatory and derogatory remarks about Bangladesh Nationalist Party lawmaker and freedom fighter Fazlur Rahman....

item.title: Jatiya Pitha Utsab o Baishakhi Mela begins today
item.description: The 19th Jatiya Pitha Utsab and Baishakhi Mela will begin today on the Bangladesh Shilpakala Academy premises in the capital....

item.title: AZM Zahid emphasises on increasing women’s economic inclusion in national policies
item.description: Social welfare and women and children affairs minister Abu Zafar Md Zahid Hossain at a programme on Thursday emphasised on increasing women’s economic inclusion in national policies and strategic plans, eliminating gender-based discrimination......
